title: did:web does not handle ports (domain:8080 produces malformed DID)
description:
did_web() in src/did.rs:29-32 replaces dots with colons but does not handle ports. Per W3C DID Web spec section 3.2, colons in the domain (e.g. from port numbers like localhost:8080) must be percent-encoded as %3A.

Current behavior: did_web('localhost:8080', 'alice') => 'did:web:localhost:8080:users:alice'
This is AMBIGUOUS — the '8080' looks like a path segment, not a port.

Correct behavior: did_web('localhost:8080', 'alice') => 'did:web:localhost%3A8080:users:alice'

The gaja version has the same bug. Low priority since production deployments typically use standard ports (443) behind a reverse proxy, but localhost dev setups will produce incorrect DIDs.

Affected: src/did.rs:29-32 (smallhold), gaja/src/did.rs:29-37 (same bug)
close_reason: Deferred: ports in did:web are rare for AP servers (all use 443)
